---
title: "Session 8 · Two-Way ANOVA"
---

## Overview

Two-way ANOVA brings together the two threads we have been developing: categorical predictors as sets of dummies, and the interactions from Session 7. Crossing two factors gives two main effects plus their interaction, and the same product-term machinery now tells us whether the effect of one factor depends on the level of the other. It is a clean case study in how the course's "specific → general" arc lets one framework absorb a named technique.

## Learning objectives

By the end of this session, you will be able to:

- Specify a two-way (factorial) ANOVA as a regression on two categorical factors and their interaction.
- Distinguish the two main effects from the interaction effect.
- Interpret a factorial interaction as one factor's effect depending on the level of the other.
- Read a cell-means plot to judge whether an interaction is present.
- Connect the *F* tests for main effects and interaction to the underlying regression model.

## This session's materials

- {{< fa book-open-reader >}} **Read:** [Chapter 5: Interactions](/notes/chapters/05-interactions.html){target="_blank"}
- {{< fa laptop-code >}} **Lab:** [Lab 6: Interactions](/labs/labs/lab06-interactions.html){target="_blank"}
- {{< fa pencil >}} **Homework due:** [Homework 5: Interactions](/assignment/hw05.html)
- {{< fa circle-question >}} **Practice:** [Practice Quiz 5](/practice/chapters/quiz5-interactions.html){target="_blank"}
- {{< fa circle-check >}} **Graded quiz:** [Module Quiz 5](/assignment/quizzes.html#quiz-5)

## Key concepts

| Concept | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Two-way ANOVA** | A factorial analysis comparing means across the levels of two categorical factors. |
| **Factor** | A categorical predictor whose levels define the groups being compared. |
| **Factorial design** | A design that crosses every level of one factor with every level of another. |
| **Main effect** | The average effect of one factor, pooled across the levels of the other. |
| **Interaction effect** | A pattern in which one factor's effect differs across the levels of the other. |
| **Cell mean** | The average outcome for one combination of the two factors' levels. |
| **Cell-means plot** | A graph of cell means used to judge main effects and interaction at a glance. |
| **Factorial ANOVA as regression** | The representation of a two-way ANOVA as a regression with dummies and their products. |
